title: quercus vulcanica
intro: Quercus vulcanica, also known as the Vulcan Oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the island of Crete in Greece. It belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus vulcanica are attributed to its ability to protect against cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and boost the immune system. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of the Vulcan Oak are used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
